config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan minoritas atau membungkam kritik. Di Indonesia, kita sering lihat alasan "demi umum" dipakai terlalu mudah, padahal itu bisa merugikan kebebasan, keadilan, dan martabat orang per orang. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as secondary to society’s interests in any blanket way. From an American perspective, I am wary of that statement because “the good of society” has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Society does matter, but its interests should be balanced against individual rights, not automatically put first. A free society is strongest when it protects people’s basic rights even when that is inconvenient for the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Pengalaman di Indonesia menunjukkan alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kritis, membatasi kebebasan, atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak dasar orang per orang, lalu mencari titik temu yang adil tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai korban yang mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my American perspective, I do not trust any blanket claim that "society first" will be used fairly, because it has too often been a cover for censorship, discrimination, and government abuse against unpopular people. At the same time, individual rights are not a license to harm others, so there are real cases where public safety and shared rules matter. For me, the right standard is that society can limit individual freedom only narrowly and for a clear, legitimate reason, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi prioritas otomatis di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal itu rawan menekan minoritas dan suara kritis. Memang benar hak individu bukan izin untuk merugikan orang lain, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasannya harus ketat, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya bukan yang selalu mendahulukan mayoritas, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar setiap orang sambil mencari kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be treated as automatically more important than individual rights. From my American perspective, I am especially suspicious of that framing because “society” is often invoked to justify censorship, discrimination, or broad government power, and history shows that minorities usually pay the price. I do think there are real cases where public safety and shared rules justify limited restrictions, but those limits have to be narrow, specific, and accountable. A healthy society protects individual rights first and then builds common purpose around them, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
